mechanism_narrative: An increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding labor income and consumption capacity, which raises aggregate demand expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not positive in greater than 55 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.5 percent signals substantial slack in the labor market that depresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.5 percent over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1.0 percent and the CPIAUCSL index rises month-over-month, the combination of accommodative nominal policy and positive inflation surprises erodes real rates and redirects capital into equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive inflation
failure_condition: During months when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0 percent and CPIAUCSL rises, 60-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative or statistically insignificant (t-test p > 0.10) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high-unemployment regimes, a month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate signals that labor market deterioration is reversing, reducing equity risk premia and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8 percent and declines from the prior month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects increasing manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports higher corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not positive in greater than 55 percent of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nominal retail sales growth exceeding 2 percent month-over-month indicates a sharp demand surprise that outpaces consensus expectations, forcing a rapid upward repricing of consumer-facing equities and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following RSAFS month-over-month growth above 2 percent are negative or zero in more than 45 percent of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls and nominal retail sales indicate a broad-based demand expansion where income growth and spending growth reinforce each other, producing stronger positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and RSAFS increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising PAYEMS and rising RSAFS does not produce higher average 20-day returns than the independent single-series signals, or the interaction term in a linear model has a t-statistic below 2.0 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP signal an economy-wide contraction in aggregate demand that compresses corporate revenues and profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following two consecutive quarterly declines in GDPC1 are positive in more than 50 percent of instances over any 5-year rolling window.
